zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas Pidge於1583年5月8日出生在英國艾可時，他的父親Nicholas Pigg Pidge31歲，母親Johanna Adame31歲。他於1619年11月1日與Mary Martha Sothy在英國艾可莎佛朗 瓦爾登結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和6個女兒的父母。他去世於1643年12月30日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可洛波利享年60歲，埋葬在美國麻薩諸塞州素福爾可波斯頓洛波利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Thomas Pidge于1583年5月8日出生在英国艾可时，他的父亲Nicholas Pigg Pidge31岁，母亲Johanna Adame31岁。他于1619年11月1日与Mary Martha Sothy在英国艾可莎佛朗 瓦爾登结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和6个女儿的父母。他去世于1643年12月30日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可洛波利享年60岁，埋葬在美国马萨诸塞州素福爾可波斯頓洛波利。

nameMeanings.name: Pidge
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: topographic name for someone who lived on or by a ridge, Middle English rigge (Old English hrycg) ‘ridge’, or a habitational name from any of the minor places so named. It is synonymous with West Midlands Rudge and northern Rigg . The surname is also fairly common in Ireland, in County Galway, having been taken to Connacht in the early 17th century. The name is sometimes Gaelicized as Mac Iomaire; iomaire being modern Irish for ‘ridge’. Compare Attridge .
sometimes perhaps a variant of   Rich   with voicing of the formerly medial consonant.

variants.name: Rigg
meanings: English: habitational name from one or other of the many minor placenames in northern England named with Middle English rigg (Old Norse hryggr) ‘ridge’. Compare the synonymous Ridge .
German: from a short form of any of several ancient Germanic personal names based on the element rīc ‘power(ful)’. This surname is very rare in Germany.
Altered form of Swiss German Rüegg (see Ruegg ).

variants.name: Ridges
meanings: English (Hampshire and Surrey): variant of Ridge , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Pidge was born on 8 May 1583, in Essex, England, his father, Nicholas Pigg Pidge, was 31 and his mother, Johanna Adame, was 31. He married Mary Martha Sothy on 1 November 1619, in Saffron Walden, Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 6 daughters. He died on 30 December 1643, in Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 60, and was buried in Roxbury, Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas Pidge est né le 8 mai 1583, à Essex, Angleterre, son père, Nicholas Pigg Pidge, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Johanna Adame, avait 31 ans. Il a épousé Mary Martha Sothy le 1 novembre 1619, à Saffron Walden, Essex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 6 filles. Il est décédé le 30 décembre 1643, à Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 60 ans, et a été enterré à Roxbury, Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts, États-Unis.

de.lifeSummary: Als Thomas Pidge am 8. Mai 1583 in Essex, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Nicholas Pigg Pidge 31 und seine Mutter Johanna Adame 31. Er hat am 1. November 1619 in Saffron Walden, Essex, England Mary Martha Sothy geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Er starb am 30. Dezember 1643 im Alter von 60 Jahren in Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America und wurde in Roxbury, Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts, Vereinigte Staaten begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Pidge è nato il il 8 maggio 1583 nel Essex, England, il suo padre, Nicholas Pigg Pidge, aveva 31 anni e il suo madre, Johanna Adame, aveva 31 anni. Lui sposato Mary Martha Sothy il 1 novembre 1619, nel Saffron Walden, Essex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 6 figlie. Morì il 30 dicembre 1643, nel Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, all'età di 60 anni, e fu sepolto a Roxbury, Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts, Stati Uniti d'America.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas Pidgeが1583年5月8日にイギリスエセックスで生まれた時，父Nicholas Pigg Pidgeは31歳，母Johanna Adameは31歳であった。1619年11月1日にイギリスエセックスサフロン ワルデンでMary Martha Sothyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼は1643年12月30日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニースフォルクロクスブリーで60歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国マサチューセッツ州スフォルクボストンロクスブリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Pidge(이)가 1583년 5월 8일 영국 에세스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Nicholas Pigg Pidge(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Johanna Adame(은)는 31세였다. 그는 1619년 11월 1일 영국 에세스 사프런 월든에서 Mary Martha Sothy(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1643년 12월 30일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 록스버이에서 60세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 매사추세츠 주 수폴크 보스턴 록스버이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Pidge nasceu em 8 de maio de 1583, em Essex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Nicholas Pigg Pidge, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Johanna Adame, tinha 31 anos. Ele casou-se com Mary Martha Sothy em 1 de novembro de 1619, em Saffron Walden, Essex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 6 filhas. Ele faleceu em 30 de dezembro de 1643, em Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, com 60 anos, e foi sepultado em Roxbury, Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas Pidge родился 8 мая 1583 г. в местности эссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени Nicholas Pigg Pidge было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Johanna Adame, 31. Он женился на женщине по имени Mary Martha Sothy 1 ноября 1619 г. в местности саффрон валден, эссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 6 дочерей. Он умер 30 декабря 1643 г. в местности роксбури, суффолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 60 лет и был похоронен в местности роксбури, бостон, суффолк, Массачусетс, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Thomas Pidge nació el 8 de mayo de 1583, en Essex, Inglaterra, su papá, Nicholas Pigg Pidge, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Johanna Adame, tenía 31 años. Se casó con Mary Martha Sothy el 1 de noviembre de 1619, en Saffron Walden, Essex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 6 hijas. Murió el 30 de diciembre de 1643, en Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 60 años, y fue sepultado en Roxbury, Boston, Suffolk, Masachusets, Estados Unidos.
